Wild blackberries on the side of the Minuteman Commuter Bikeway. Half a mile from Wiggins Ave crossing. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: August-September.
A tall stand of elderberries beyond the out field fence of the baseball fields. Easy parking. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: August-September.
Black Walnut tree with branches hanging down within reach. Easily accessible from Battle Rd. Trail. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: September-October.
Lots of blackberries -- but watch out for poison ivy! Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: July-August.
120 Bartlett Hill Rd, Concord, MA 01742, USA

Highbush Blueberry located along the "high road" on the main trail close to Sleepy Hollow, and on the "flooded trail" which cuts across the wetland area. Multiple bushes interspersed with other plants. Productivity is fair, but lots of birds means competition.

2 elderberry bushes with excellent yield. You can find them in the overgrown bushed along the trail, but they are fairly hidden. This is a working farm, managed by Concord's Natural Resources Division and the area gets cut back regularly. Access can be found a

Loads of Concord Grape best to harvest in mid September. You can park on Dudley Road near fire lane and follow sign to Brothers Rocks. Then follow River bank north. You will smell them! Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: September-October.
Multiple black walnut along tree line near trail. Mostly high branches out of reach, but walnuts will still drop and be good pickings. Access: Public.

High yield Concord Grapes along trail at the back of the fields. Trail loops along the boundary behind the "meadow area" and grapes are interspersed on the tree line. More in the southern side of the trail, but some more north and close to ground level. Access

Multiple overgrown raspberries and blackcaps in a massive wall of thorns. Fair productivity but surrounded by invasive multiflora rose. Managed by Concord Conservation Department, so this is regularly cut back. Access: Public.
